qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote file pull SSRF blocklist omits special-use IP ranges and local wildcard destinations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a cron-driven SQLite activity-log deletion bug in Wings; this finding concerns the remote file-pull downloader's incomplete internal-address filtering and does not touch the activity upload or delete path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SFTP Setstat follows attacker-created symlinks and chmods host files outside the server root
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about oversized activity-log deletion batches in cron processing; this finding instead targets SFTP symlink creation plus Setstat-driven chmod following in filesystem code, which is a different component and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described activity-log reprocessing flaw caused by exceeding SQLite's parameter limit during cron-driven deletion of processed entries.
evidence: The advisory describes `internal/cron/activity_cron.go` and `internal/cron/sftp_cron.go` building an oversized SQLite delete operation, which leaves processed activity rows undeleted and causes endless re-upload to the panel.
The `remote_pull_ssrf_special_ip_range` finding targets `router_server_files.go` and `downloader.go`, where a remote file-pull URL filter omits some internal destinations.
The `sftp_setstat_symlink_chmod_escape` finding targets `sftp/handler.go` and `internal/ufs/fs_unix.go`, where chmod follows attacker-created symlinks to host paths.
Neither qualifying finding references the advisory's activity-log cron pipeline, SQLite parameter limit, failed deletion query, or repeated panel activity upload behavior.
